description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a wooden cabinet exterior and a prominent convex glass screen. The front features two large dials and a speaker grille on the right side, with a black plastic bezel framing the screen. Two metal V-shaped antennae extend from the top, and a power cord with a plug trails from the back. The television stands on four slender, angled metal legs, giving it a mid-century modern appearance. The rear panel is plastic with ventilation grilles and molded details, and the overall design is boxy with a deep profile typical of CRT technology.
description: A vintage wooden television with a convex glass screen, metal antennae, and angled metal legs, featuring front dials and a rear power cord.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wooden cabinet's depth, the protruding screen, and the power cord trailing from the back.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the convex glass screen, two control dials, speaker grille, and the V-shaped antennae.
description_view_2: Opposite side view emphasizing the wood grain and the television's boxy profile.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the plastic back panel with ventilation grilles, antennae bases, and the power cord.
attribute_bezel_design: thick black plastic bezel with a rectangular shape and rounded corners
attribute_control_button_layout: two large dials and a speaker grille on the front right side, arranged vertically
